markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Allow context bundles to cite UserManual pages with version and source anchors. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualContextBundleBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualContextBundleBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Context bundle bridge persists UserManual citations with slug, version, anchor, and content hash metadata; real PostgreSQL proof and adversarial review PASS.

evidence.summary: UserManual bundle bridge exposes manual pages as retrieval/context-bundle citation candidates with manual version, section anchor, and content hash metadata.
MT-202 targeted content test passed against real PostgreSQL: compiled context bundle cites state-recovery-guide with slug, manual version, section anchor, and content-hash prefix.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.

Heisenberg read-only adversarial review returned PASS for MT-202. Non-blocking review gap: the MT-202 test proves PostgreSQL bundle persistence and citation shape but does not directly query an EventLedger row for this bridge.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-202 (UserManualContextBundleBridge) requires that context bundles can cite UserManual pages with version + source anchors. The committed code implements exactly this: it mirrors a manual page into a real knowledge_entities row and emits a bundle candidate whose citation carries slug@version#anchor@hash, and a real PostgreSQL-gated test compiles a real context bundle, persists it, reloads it, and asserts the persisted citation shape. Proof is real, not mock/placeholder.
findings: src/backend/handshake_core/src/user_manual/bundle_bridge.rs:30-94 — real MT-202 deliverable: manual_citation_base() builds 'usermanual:<slug>@<version>#<anchor>'; ensure_manual_page_entity() upserts a knowledge_entities row of kind user_manual_page (idempotent); manual_bundle_candidate() emits a BundleCandidate with EvidenceSnippet carrying content_sha256 (drift hash) and the citation base. Directly satisfies MT scope 'cite UserManual pages with version and source anchors'.

src/backend/handshake_core/tests/user_manual_content_tests.rs:354-441 mt202_bundle_cites_manual_page_with_version_and_anchor — REAL runtime proof: real CheapestAuthoritativePathPlanner + ContextBundleCompilerV2 compile a bundle, persist to PG, reload via get_knowledge_context_bundle, then assert the persisted citation contains slug+'@', manual_version, '#<section_kind>-<position>' anchor, and content_hash[..8] drift prefix, plus bundle target kind user_manual_page. Idempotency of entity id is also asserted (lines 372-375).

src/backend/handshake_core/tests/user_manual_support.rs:132-142 skip_if_no_pg! PANICS when PostgreSQL is unavailable ('UserManual proof requires live PostgreSQL/EventLedger') — it does NOT silently skip, so the MT-202 test cannot pass vacuously without real PG.

src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/knowledge.rs:3874-3913 upsert_knowledge_entity is a real Postgres SQL INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (workspace_id, entity_kind, entity_key) DO UPDATE upsert — the Handshake-owned durable resource the bridge touches (sub-rule 2 satisfied).

Enum variants are real, not invented stubs: KnowledgeEntityKind::UserManualPage (storage/knowledge.rs:845) and BundleTargetKind::UserManualPage (knowledge_retrieval/compiler.rs:57) both map to 'user_manual_page'.
No placeholder/escape hits (todo!/unimplemented!/*Unavailable/deferred/pending MT-) in bundle_bridge.rs or the MT-202 test (sub-rule 1).
lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260614-175624, completed_by=null, status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION — no self-certified COMPLETED transition (sub-rule 3).
Both deliverable files are git-tracked (committed in fcf9bce3); changes confined to src/backend/handshake_core; no SQLite/Docker/external-search forbidden-path usage.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): committed evidence log green-mt202-context-bundle-proof.log not present on disk; packet spec_bundle cites v02.193 vs current v02.194 — neither affects code correctness.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
